name: Dulce Base
notes: Schneider claimed to have helped build. Alleged joint human/non-human facility. 7 subterranean levels. Schneider died under suspicious circumstances Dec 1996.

name: Area 51 / Groom Lake
notes: Existence officially acknowledged 2013. CIA declassified history confirms U-2/OXCART/F-117 testing. Underground facilities widely reported by former personnel.

name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
notes: Manhattan Project origin. Extensive underground testing and storage facilities. Active nuclear weapons research.

name: Sandia National Laboratories
notes: Nuclear weapons engineering center. Manzano Base adjacent — confirmed underground nuclear weapons storage since 1950s.

name: Denver International Airport
depth_estimate: Multiple sub-levels confirmed, deeper levels alleged
notes: Opened 1995, $2B over budget. Confirmed underground baggage system. Murals depicting apocalyptic scenes. Multiple construction anomalies documented. Workers reported being rotated to prevent knowledge of full scope.

name: Mount Weather Emergency Operations Center
notes: FEMA continuity of government facility. Underground city with own power, water, transit system. Used on 9/11. Part of COG network.

name: Raven Rock Mountain Complex (Site R)
notes: Alternate Pentagon. Built 1950-53. Underground city inside granite mountain. Cheney operated from here on 9/11. Expansion projects ongoing.

name: Cheyenne Mountain Complex
notes: NORAD/USNORTHCOM. Built 1961-66. 15 buildings on springs inside mountain. Blast doors 25 tons each. Placed on 'warm standby' 2006, reactivated 2015.

name: Pine Gap (Joint Defence Facility)
notes: Joint US/Australian intelligence facility. Largest ECHELON station in Southern Hemisphere. Satellite ground control. Underground levels acknowledged but details classified.

name: White Sands Missile Range
notes: Largest military installation in US (3,200 sq mi). First nuclear detonation (Trinity). Underground testing facilities. Adjacent to Holloman AFB.

name: Edwards Air Force Base
notes: Air Force Flight Test Center. Extensive underground facilities reported by former contractors. Connected to classified aerospace programs.

name: Vandenberg Space Force Base
notes: Space launch facility. Underground missile silos (decommissioned ICBMs). Active satellite launch operations. Space Force Western Range.

name: Camp David (Naval Support Facility Thurmont)
notes: Presidential retreat since FDR. Underground bunker facilities. Part of COG network with Mt. Weather and Raven Rock.

name: Manzano Mountain Nuclear Weapons Storage
notes: Nuclear weapons storage facility since 1950. Tunnels bored into Manzano Mountain. Adjacent to Kirtland AFB/Sandia Labs. Partially decommissioned but underground infrastructure remains.

name: Wright-Patterson AFB
notes: National Air and Space Intelligence Center. Foreign Technology Division. Long-standing reports of retrieved material storage (Hangar 18 lore). Sen. Barry Goldwater confirmed denied access to classified section.

name: The Greenbrier Bunker
notes: Congressional relocation facility built 1958. 112,000 sq ft beneath Greenbrier resort. Maintained in secret for 30 years. Decommissioned after 1992 Washington Post exposure. Now public tours.

name: Nellis AFB / Nevada Test & Training Range
notes: Encompasses Nevada National Security Site (formerly NTS). 928 nuclear tests created extensive underground infrastructure. Yucca Mountain adjacent.

name: Dugway Proving Ground
notes: Chemical/biological defense testing. 800,000 acres in Utah desert. Life Sciences Division. Multiple restricted underground facilities.

name: Peterson SFB (NORAD Alternate)
notes: NORAD and USNORTHCOM headquarters since 2006. Alternate command center after Cheyenne Mountain went to warm standby.

name: Archuleta Mesa
notes: Adjacent to Dulce. Bennewitz (electronics specialist near Kirtland) claimed to intercept signals. AFOSI involvement (Richard Doty). Possible disinformation operation complicates assessment.
